recentTweets.text:
We can confirm the real identity of the man from our story last Wednesday as Salama Mohammed Salama

https://t.co/wb77EEMbnT
The man from our report, reunited with a family member.
This is 100% not Austin Tice. I have no idea who it is but it is not Austin.

In nearly twenty years as a journalist, this was one of the most extraordinary moments I have witnessed. 

https://t.co/rG3WmhKh7X

Two weeks ago, our team was held captive by a militia in Darfur. As a journalist, one never wants to become the story. But I wanted to write this because in some ways our experience tells a bigger story of the complexities of the situation in Darfur and the challenges of getting
Extraordinary. What is the official reason for this ban from Germany that apparently applies to the entire Shengen area?
"If they decide to kill me, it means that we are incredibly strong" - a chilling message from Alexei Navalny before he returned to Russia.
Grateful for this honor

They were waving white flags and then they were shot. Our investigation into the shooting of unarmed civilians in Gaza. The video is graphic but please watch. With @BrentSwailsCNN Kareem Khadder, @elizamackintosh @GianlucaMezzo @ahmedhijazee 

https://t.co/rtX1bG0JLf
Courage and grace. The work of journalists in Gaza like Bisan is vital and heroic.

Our hour long special “The Will to Win: Ukraine at War” will air Sunday night at 8pm ET (for those in US) and Monday at 9pm BST (for those in Europe and MENA). Please tune in! Grateful to an amazing team and to the extraordinary Ukrainians who opened up their lives to us. 🙏🏼💗 https://t.co/nRMQ41m4Qw

I now have dozens of accounts that I do not follow popping up in my timeline because people who I do follow have liked something they tweeted. Does anyone find this a bit confusing/ overwhelming? I am a simple creature. Will I grow to love this new feature?

I will keep you posted on how I get on and am blown away by the kindness and generosity of so many of you offering support and guidance. Thank you. It should not be so hard for anyone to get appropriate treatment for their kid.

UPDATE: within 2 hours of posting these tweets, someone from CIGNA called my home. On the one hand, it’s a step in the right direction. On the other hand, I am keenly aware that people who don’t have a public profile/ social media following probably don’t get the same treatment.
@clancynewyork @Cigna @audiosand This is so crazy and disturbing - very relieved the scan was approved ultimately
@Cignaquestions I have emailed you countless times in the past to no avail and forgive me if a generic email address doesn’t feel like meaningful customer service but I will email you now.

Just spent 45 minutes on hold with @Cigna for the second time today and call was dropped for the second time today. Honestly, I feel broken. This is not a way to treat people. Please get in touch if you have had similar experiences or have any wisdom/ suggestions to impart

Before I enrolled my son in this therapy, I called @Cigna to make sure it was fully covered (it was) and now I can't get a straight answer as to why they won't reimburse me. We are tens of thousands of dollars out of pocket.

Does anyone have any ideas or has been in a similar situation? I feel like I am losing my mind. The thing that @Cigna does is that they don't refuse to repay you, they just say "pending" and with there's an "N/A" with explanation of benefits. FOR SEVEN MONTHS.

For 7 months @Cigna hasn't reimbursed routine therapy for my son who has special needs. For 7 months, they have stonewalled me about why, even though the therapy is clearly covered in my policy. For 7 months, I have sat on hold, shuttled from operator to operator. At my wit's end
Sharing some news for those who may not know https://t.co/vEvZsT0iWR
This is fascinating. Who is she? A bodyguard? An actor? There are several other faces who appear in both photos on the right.
This was one of the more painful interviews I have done. Tatiana was brave enough to share her story but many more are not.

We were given exclusive access to an Iranian-made Mohajer-6 drone, shot down a few weeks ago by Ukrainian forces in Southern Ukraine. This as the Kremlin today claimed Russia is only using "Russian weapons with Russian numbers" in its "special operation." More tonight on CNN. https://t.co/FNM97EaHtk

The Taliban has declared today a national holiday to commemorate the “liberation from American occupation” but few are celebrating here in Kabul. One year on, Afghanistan is poorer and more isolated. Girls cannot go to school after sixth grade. https://t.co/85IyKsLPFd

The great @HalaGorani is saying goodbye to @cnn for now. She exemplifies the calm, astute, humility and humanity that so many of us aspire to. Tenacious, fair, thoughtful and reflective- she will be sorely missed! https://t.co/U7Ylrpmkyf
Slightly ominous end to the segment. “Of course we hope that Clarissa gets out of Ukraine safely and alive.”
If you’re not already following this extraordinary, courageous woman, start now.
Don’t miss this extraordinary documentary tonight (with a very small cameo from yours truly). Congrats to Daniel Roher Odessa Rae @christogrozev @pevchikh

Perhaps the most extraordinary moment in our story today was when an evacuated couple shouted, “Putin wants a peaceful solution… he wanted to negotiate.” To which the volunteer who rescued then snapped back “don’t  tell this bullsh$t to the whole world.”

Ukrainian commander Maj Serhii Volyna trapped in Mariupol’s Azovstal steel plant tells CNN the situation is “critical” and urges a third country to help broker an extraction agreement. “We have only a few days or a few hours.” He implores President Biden and the US to help.

To the hundreds of you who have sent messages of concern about 86 year old Lidia, with offers to help, she has been safely evacuated from Avdiivka and is here in Dnipro. A rare piece of good news amid so much horror and heartache. https://t.co/gSzFGNGWPl
“I never imagined that my end would be like this. You can’t even die here because there’s no one to provide a burial ceremony.”
No words. Just pure agony. Extremely disturbing video.
Good to be back #Kyiv https://t.co/az4851rxmU

There are no words. I had the great privilege of working with Pierre and the even greater privilege of calling him a friend. An extraordinary spirit and tremendous talent and one of the kindest, most gracious colleagues on the road. Absolutely heartbreaking.
The courage of these people just beggars belief.
No words
Even if you don’t speak Italian, please take a moment to watch this incredible reporting from @mannocchia who witnessed some of the youngest and most vulnerable of Vorzel’s citizens being evacuated.
Endless horror in Mariupol.
This is at least the fourth incident of press being targeted.

Horrifying. The bridge in Irpin where we did our story on civilians being evacuated has been hit by mortars today. 25 seconds into this clip. Feel sick. People were continuing to flee today. https://t.co/mGAmznFbJI

Please watch our story on the desperate plight of Ukrainians who have been pinned down for days in Kyiv suburb Irpin under heavy bombardment. With Brent Swails, Scott McWhinnie, Denis Lapin and Mike Pratt.

Our story from Ukraine’s largest children’s hospital, where the sickest patients are now being in treated in basement hallways. So hard to see sick children in any setting but this is just heartbreaking https://t.co/fw1g0IUWA0
“Russia is not Putin,” says imprisoned Russian opposition leader Alexey Navalny. He calls for acts of civil disobedience.
Congratulations to @bbclysedoucet, one of the most gracious and gutsy journalists out there.

Joke making the rounds in Russia right now:
“The West really wants this war badly. If Russia doesn’t attack Ukraine before wednesday, there'll be sanctions.”
Such good news

A picture is worth a thousand words. You don’t have to zoom in to see how the Taliban’s head of the ministry for the propagation of virtue and prevention of vice in Ghazni feels about sitting down with me. Our story here. https://t.co/Wh7jMkbXjx https://t.co/RzqIzg8nqb

1) Not a day goes by, when I am not approached or contacted by multiple Afghans who are desperately waiting for news on their SIV applications. Most of them were filed long before the Taliban took over. Almost all of them worked for the Americans for many years.
No words.    https://t.co/qIvQzhU1Ig

Can I just post one thing about Afghanistan w/o getting a slew of defensive msgs that “we shed enough blood there.” It is possible to support ending the war and also to have empathy for people in Afghanistan. Stop politicising everything and be a human being for a minute!

From protests being broken up with machine gun fire, to 12 year old girls being banned from school- women in Afghanistan are being erased from public life. Our report on the ground in Kabul about the women who are fighting the Taliban with small acts of great courage.

Tremendous honour to be awarded 2 #NewsEmmys for our investigation into the poisoning of @navalny with the brilliant @bellingcat. Huge thanks to @christogrozev  @TimListerCNN @sebshukla @EliotHiggins Dasha Tarasova, Jeff Kehl, Oscar Featherstone, Christian Streib and Michael Pohl
Ummmm

Taliban source tells CNN they’re concerned ISIS K fighters have “been melted” with the Taliban in Kabul and it’s very challenging to distinguish them from legitimate Taliban fighters. This makes securing the capital a challenge.

Some rare good news. I interviewed @FawziaKoofi77 while she was under house arrest in Kabul with Taliban guards outside her door. She was a picture of courage and composure. Today she’s reunited with her daughter, thanks to the efforts of Qatar and many others.
So. Much. Heartache.

Talking to a woman, a US citizen, who worked as a translator for the US for more than ten years, who was not able to get into the Kabul airport yet again today. She will try again tomorrow but is completely terrified.
Horror doesn’t begin to describe it.

kurdistanMentions.text: My book comes out on Tuesday. It’s about 15 years covering conflict + crises around the world. Unsurprisingly, much of it is about Syria. More broadly, it’s a love letter to journalism + to the extraordinary people I’ve crossed paths with along the way. #onallfronts https://t.co/XvY0Zc5ubf
Outside #Kurdish town of #RasAlAin after Turkish strikes earlier- please watch
Women and children squashed into flatbed trucks told me they didn’t know where they were fleeing to - “where is safe to go?” #syria #kurds
On the outskirts of #RasAlAin earlier following Turkish bombardment of the town. We are the only US network here in #syria . https://t.co/BT9Fh5PCzG
One Syrian tells me the new nom de guerre for President Trump on the ground in Northern Syria is Abu Ivanka al Amriki. #syria

assessment.summary: The captured X archive has 5 Kurdistan Lens tweet hits for Clarissa Ward. The social-media posture reads as direct but mixed kurdistan signal because the strongest matched frame is direct Kurdistan/Kurdish-region references without a single dominant frame. Top matched terms: Syria / Rojava, Kurds / Kurdish.
assessment.whatItWasLike: When this account mentioned the Kurdish file, the language most often clustered around direct Kurdistan/Kurdish-region references without a single dominant frame. I count 2 direct Kurdistan/Kurdish-region term hits, 0 security-frame hits, 1 supportive or diplomatic hit, 0 humanitarian/minority hits, and 0 energy/economic hits in the exported profile window. The latest matched tweet is dated 2020-09-03T21:46:30Z and should be opened before treating this as a final stance.
assessment.dominantFrame: direct Kurdistan/Kurdish-region references without a single dominant frame

1) Not a day goes by, when I am not approached or contacted by multiple Afghans who are desperately waiting for news on their SIV applications. Most of them were filed long before the Taliban took over. Almost all of them worked for the Americans for many years.
No words.    https://t.co/qIvQzhU1Ig

Can I just post one thing about Afghanistan w/o getting a slew of defensive msgs that “we shed enough blood there.” It is possible to support ending the war and also to have empathy for people in Afghanistan. Stop politicising everything and be a human being for a minute!

From protests being broken up with machine gun fire, to 12 year old girls being banned from school- women in Afghanistan are being erased from public life. Our report on the ground in Kabul about the women who are fighting the Taliban with small acts of great courage.

Tremendous honour to be awarded 2 #NewsEmmys for our investigation into the poisoning of @navalny with the brilliant @bellingcat. Huge thanks to @christogrozev  @TimListerCNN @sebshukla @EliotHiggins Dasha Tarasova, Jeff Kehl, Oscar Featherstone, Christian Streib and Michael Pohl
Ummmm

Taliban source tells CNN they’re concerned ISIS K fighters have “been melted” with the Taliban in Kabul and it’s very challenging to distinguish them from legitimate Taliban fighters. This makes securing the capital a challenge.

Some rare good news. I interviewed @FawziaKoofi77 while she was under house arrest in Kabul with Taliban guards outside her door. She was a picture of courage and composure. Today she’s reunited with her daughter, thanks to the efforts of Qatar and many others.
So. Much. Heartache.

Talking to a woman, a US citizen, who worked as a translator for the US for more than ten years, who was not able to get into the Kabul airport yet again today. She will try again tomorrow but is completely terrified.
Horror doesn’t begin to describe it.

Can anyone recommend information resources for Afghans looking to get out? I am getting a slew of messages every day from people who have relatives in the US, who worked for the US or the UN and I am unable to answer their questions about what the best process is for them. Thanks

I witnessed several moments like this at Kabul airport. A female soldier carrying a weeping Afghan toddler. A Marine supporting an elderly man as he struggled to walk. Servicemen rushing a dehydrated newborn to get medical attention. So many working so hard.

Just landed in Doha with the team and nearly 300 Afghan evacuees. Huge thanks to all of you for your support and concern, to the US Air Force for flying us out and to Qatar for welcoming us. We are the lucky ones.
On our flight and getting ready for takeoff https://t.co/bGaYREsbxT
Response from Qatar- emphatic that evacuations will continue.

The fourth bird we were supposed to get on just got pulled. Going to be a long night here, especially for those we are talking to who have been here since yesterday evening. A woman just asked me for a blanket so I gave her my scarf.

Afghan evacuees try to sleep on the gravel with C17’s in the background. It’s a chilly night and incredibly loud, especially after 14 hours. One Afghan acquaintance tells me “it’s an insult to human dignity… I don’t know why the Americans are doing this.” Photo @BrentSwailsCNN https://t.co/iqXl1nFkiY

Soldiers by the runway at Kabul airport tell me that there are 10,000 people here processed and ready to go… but nowhere to fly them to because Qatar is refusing to accept more Afghans because they’ve reached capacity. “It’s abysmal… someone needs to step up.”

Our story from the madness outside Kabul airport where we witnessed Taliban fighters whipping people, shooting into the crowd and amazing producer @BrentSwailsCNN had a very close call https://t.co/YJZKCdCFAa
In awe of these women.
Courage

This meme is inaccurate. The top photo is inside a private compound. The bottom is on the streets of Taliban held Kabul. I always wore a head scarf on the street in Kabul previously, though not w/ hair fully covered and abbaya. So there is a difference but not quite this stark. https://t.co/BmIRFFSdSE
On the streets of Kabul today- feel we are witnessing history https://t.co/wcVKzbT6oJ
Please watch our story from FOB Andar in Ghazni Province. Once a US base, now under the control of the Taliban. What it means for America’s legacy and Afghanistan’s future https://t.co/KiG5ztG6c8
Very hard to watch.
Please watch our story on the heartbreaking and horrifying tragedy taking place in India right now #IndiaCovidCrisis
So enjoyed this conversation
Our latest story on #WhatsHappeninglnMyanmar
To friends and followers celebrating in around the world, Ramadan Mubarak. https://t.co/R5qfcM9jcJ
Our story from Myanmar coming up in just over 10 minutes on @TheLeadCNN - don’t miss it
Late to this incredibly powerful story

Very striking that I am being absolutely inundated with positive, heartfelt messages from people in Myanmar, while a handful of white male academics/ commentators (none of them in the country) write endless screeds about how offensive my trip is to the people of Myanmar 🤔

To the people of #Myanmar- we couldn’t seek you out while we were in your country. Please know that we heard your voices, we saw your pain and your courage and we will do everything we can to do justice to your story. 🙏🏼🙏🏼🙏🏼
#ontheroad #myanmar https://t.co/OR2eaLNRip

Navalny knew that if he stayed in Europe, he would cease to be relevant as a Russian politician. He also full understood the risk of going back. But as he told us, “I would never give Putin such a gift” by staying outside Russia.
This is awful

Another extraordinary bombshell to drop here. Russian opposition leader Alexey Navalny duped FSB agent into confessing details of the poisoning operation that was supposed to kill him. Jaw dropping. https://t.co/BZY1uiaJcW
Not gonna lie, it’s a real relief to be taxiing the runway out of Moscow’s Sheremyetovo airport. What a crazy week. What an amazing team. See you on the other side.

My book comes out on Tuesday. It’s about 15 years covering conflict + crises around the world. Unsurprisingly, much of it is about Syria. More broadly, it’s a love letter to journalism + to the extraordinary people I’ve crossed paths with along the way. #onallfronts https://t.co/XvY0Zc5ubf
Some exciting news to share!!!

Thoughts from CNN takedown of a Russian troll farm operating out of Ghana and Nigeria.

1) Alarming that a group of 24 kids working on Samsung Galaxy’s (no laptops) at a big table were able to reach more than a quarter of a million people, mostly Americans, in just a few months https://t.co/PUJZn2z3pm

1/ EXCLUSIVE: Russian trolls have outsourced their disinformation campaigns to Ghana and Nigeria, focusing on racial issues in the US ahead of the presidential election

https://t.co/6suLjuXhmi
Outside #Kurdish town of #RasAlAin after Turkish strikes earlier- please watch
Women and children squashed into flatbed trucks told me they didn’t know where they were fleeing to - “where is safe to go?” #syria #kurds
As we entered the town we found chaotic scenes with hundreds of civilians fleeing - streets choked with traffic and smoke from strikes https://t.co/MUjL4KcOYS
On the outskirts of #RasAlAin earlier following Turkish bombardment of the town. We are the only US network here in #syria . https://t.co/BT9Fh5PCzG
1) Amazing how many men lashing out on Twitter that I, or other women who had heard about sexual misconduct at @ABC are somehow responsible
One Syrian tells me the new nom de guerre for President Trump on the ground in Northern Syria is Abu Ivanka al Amriki. #syria
